data.title: Field Solutions Holdings Sells Unit for AU$5 Million
data.description:
Field Solutions Holdings has entered into a binding heads of agreement to sell its mining customer base to PIT Mining IT, according to a Tuesday Australian bourse filing.

The deal has a sale value of about AU$5 million, subject to working capital and other adjustments, of which about AU$2 million is subject to performance conditions, the filing added.

The company expects a reduction of AU$4.8 million in operating expenses and AU$12 million in gross revenue associated with the sale as the company aims to reduce revenue concentration and high capital expenditure risk.

The sale is subject to the finalization of a business sale agreement on or before March 1, the filing said.

data.title: Iron Ore Rises Amid Improving Sentiment — Market Talk
data.description: Iron ore prices are higher amid better sentiment. BHP, the world's largest listed miner, flagged tentative signs of recovery in the Chinese property sector, ANZ Research says in a commentary, noting that its CEO Mike Henry said that they are seeing "green shoots" in China's long-beaten property sector. Investor sentiment is boosted after Chinese President Xi Jinping's meeting with the country's tech leaders earlier this week, with Xi having urged them to maintain a "competitive spirit," signaling Beijing's support of the private sector, ANZ says. The most-traded iron ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is 1.2% higher at 818.00 yuan a ton. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: TDX Strategies Announces Structured Products Linked to CoinDesk 20 Index
data.description:
HONG KONG - Quant-driven digital assets trading firm TDX Strategies has teamed up with CoinDesk Indices for its latest offering: structured products linked to the CoinDesk 20 Index (CD 20), the company announced at Consensus Hong Kong.

The collaboration will provide investors with a sophisticated solution to prioritize both growth and risk management while navigating the burgeoning digital assets market, said TDX Chief Executive Officer Dick Lo.

"Our new offering allows investors to gain diversified exposure and participate in the growth of the digital asset market while maintaining a balanced risk profile. This sets a new standard in digital asset investment solutions," Lo said.

"By integrating the CoinDesk 20 Index into our existing product suite, TDX Strategies continues to redefine the structured product landscape in the digital asset ecosystem," Lo added.

Structured products are pre-packaged investment strategies that combine financial assets with derivatives to create a customized financial instrument, providing tailored exposure per investors' specific needs and objectives.

TDX’s new offering will essentially provide a pre-packaged strategy tied to the CoinDesk 20 Index. The firm’s present product suit includes bespoke strategy, directional exposure and yield enhancement.

The CoinDesk 20 Index, designed for scalability, tracks the performance of top digital assets utilizing a market cap-weighted methodology that provides a diversified exposure beyond bitcoin and ether.

Since its debut in January 2024, the index has gained notable traction among institutions, generating a trading volume of roughly $13 billion.

"The CoinDesk 20 has been embraced by leading market-making firms, unlocking the digital asset opportunity in one step. With substantial institutional interest driving trading volumes of approximately $13 billion since its January 2024 launch, we’re pleased that TDX Strategies will provide access through structured products," said Alan Campbell, President of CoinDesk Indices.

data.title: Asian stocks stutter as Trump's tariff vows weigh
data.description:
Key points:
  • Asian stocks waver as tariff threats sour sentiment
  • Hong Kong stocks slip on profit taking
  • Benchmark S&P 500 reaches all-time high
  • Gold chases record high touched last week
  • Investors wait on U.S-Russia talks

Asian stocks wavered on Wednesday after the S&P 500 and European shares ended at record highs, in the face of U.S President Donald Trump's latest tariff threats on auto, semiconductor and pharmaceutical imports.

Since Trump's inauguration four weeks ago, Trump has imposed a 10% tariff on all imports from China, on top of existing levies. He has also announced, and delayed for a month, 25% tariffs on goods from Mexico and non-energy imports from Canada.

Trump told reporters on Tuesday that sectoral tariffs on pharmaceuticals and semiconductor chips would start at "25% or higher, and it will go very substantially higher over the course of a year." He intends to impose similar tariffs on autos as soon as April 2.

Market reaction to Trump's threats were muted as investors increasingly see them as bargaining tools, although the U.S. dollar was on the front foot as geopolitical worries, including tense Russia-Ukraine negotiations boosted safe-haven flows.

"I think people are still trying to digest everything going on with not only tariffs and how that could impact things but also general valuations," said Sandy Villere, portfolio manager at Villere & Co in New Orleans. "We feel like the market is pretty expensive."

"You've got not only the tariff situation, which I think is going to be more sabre-rattling and negotiating than anything long-term," Villere said.

MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) fell 0.29% after touching a three-month high earlier in the session.

Chinese tech stocks have been on a tear recently as the emergence of AI startup DeepSeek and a meeting between Xi Jinping and business leaders in the sector lift sentiment.

"Green shoots are emerging in China’s economy and DeepSeek is injecting a shot of adrenaline into the sector," said Thomas Rupf, co-head Singapore and CIO Asia at VP Bank.

"While trade risks persist, tech optimism remains strong as the prospect of low-cost AI applications drives a reassessment of growth potential."

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 0.3% as investors pocketed some profits. The index has risen over 13% so far in 2025, jostling with Germany's DAX index for best-performing market in the world.

KIWI CLIPPED

The New Zealand dollar fell 0.35% to $0.5684 after the country's central bank flagged a steeper trajectory for future rate reductions following a 50 basis-point cut to 3.75% on Wednesday.

The latest easing was in line with expectations but the central bank is now forecasting the cash rate will drop to 3.4% in the second quarter of 2025 and to 3.1% by the end of 2025, lower than November's expectations.

The Australian dollar eased 0.11% to $0.6347 a day after the central bank delivered its first rate cut since 2020, but cautioned about the prospects for further easing.

Overnight, the U.S. benchmark S&P 500 <.SPX > squeaked past its previous record closing high as all three Wall Street indexes seesawed between gains and losses for much of the session before rising in the closing minutes.

European shares ended at a record high, with banking and defence stocks among the top gainers. The pan-European STOXX 600 index hit an all-time high of 557.96.

European leaders vowed to step up support for Ukraine as the U.S. and Russia held bilateral talks on the war this week. Investors also hope this weekend's German election will lead to economic stimulus.

Minutes from the U.S. Fed's January meeting, when the central bank held borrowing costs at 4.25% to 4.5%, are due later on Wednesday. That follows hawkish comments from Fed Chair Jerome Powell in testimony to Congress last week and hot consumer price data.

Brent crude oil rose 0.11% to $75.92 a barrel as traders awaited the outcome of the U.S.-Russia talks in Riyadh and speculated about potential supply increases if Washington agrees to abandon sanctions on Russian oil.

Spot gold was little changed at $2,933 an ounce, stalking the record high it touched last week on safe haven demand.

data.title: Japan chip materials maker Resonac looking to make deals, says CEO
data.description:
Japan's Resonac Holdings is looking to make deals after reducing its borrowing and wants to be involved when a state-backed fund exits from peer JSR, the CEO of the chips materials maker said on Wednesday.

"We will go on the offensive from this year," Hidehito Takahashi told Reuters in an interview.

JSR has said it wants to drive consolidation in the industry and was taken private by a state-backed fund last year.

"It's a fund so there will be an exit in the end," said Takahashi.

Resonac wants to be involved in that exit, he said.

data.title: Ethan Allen Interiors CEO Sells Over $300K in Company Stock
data.description:
Reporter NameKathwari M Farooq
RelationshipChairman, President & CEO, 10% Owner
TypeSell
Amount$313,189
SEC FilingForm 4

Ethan Allen Interiors Chairman, President & CEO, and 10% owner, Kathwari M Farooq, reported selling 10,700 shares of common stock on February 18, 2025, at a price of $29.27 per share, totaling $313,189. The sales were conducted both directly and indirectly through trusts for family members. Following these transactions, Kathwari directly owns 1,641,905 shares and indirectly owns 117,617 shares of Ethan Allen Interiors.

SEC Filing: ETHAN ALLEN INTERIORS INC [ ETD ] - Form 4 - Feb. 18, 2025

data.title: Copper Lower Amid Tariff Concerns — Market Talk
data.description: Copper is lower in early Asian trade. Supply is slightly tight while demand is stable, Nanhua Futures analysts say in a commentary. However, investors are closely watching tariff developments amid concerns that rising trade tensions could weigh on copper prices. BHP, the world's biggest miner by market value, has warned that potential trade tensions present a risk to the recovery in developed economies and across the globe, say ANZ research analysts in a commentary. The three-month LME copper contract is 0.3% lower at $9,439.00 a ton. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Singtel's Net Profit Surges 183% in Fiscal Q3
data.description:
Singapore Telecommunications' net profit surged 183% in the fiscal third quarter ended Dec 31, 2024 to SG$1.32 billion from SG$465 million a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

Operating revenue was up 1% year over year to SG$3.63 billion from SG$3.59 billion, driven by strong performances by Optus and NCS.

Analysts polled by Visible Alpha estimated a revenue of SG$3.62 billion for the period.

The telecommunications company expects to pay a total ordinary dividend of SG$0.165 per share for the year ending March 31.

data.title: St George Mining Shareholders Approve Acquisition of Brazilian Niobium Project
data.description:
St George Mining said all resolutions to enable the acquisition of the Araxá niobium project in Brazil were approved by shareholders at its general meeting, according to a Tuesday Australian bourse filing.

The company expects to complete the acquisition in the coming days, with the financial completion of the transaction scheduled for Feb 24, the filing said.

Company shares rose 2% in recent trade.

data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Healthcare Stocks Rise, Financials Stocks Plunge
data.description:
Healthcare stocks rose a little past 0.7% at midday Wednesday. Citing easing inflation, the Reserve Bank of Australia cut its key policy rate to 4.10 % from 4.35%

EBOS Group reported fiscal half-year diluted earnings of AU$0.569 per share, down from AU$0.71 per share a year earlier. Revenue for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$5.99 billion, down 9% from AU$6.58 billion a year earlier.

On the flip side, financial stocks fell over 1.7%, after National Australia Bank's cash earnings fell year-over-year.

The bank reported unaudited cash earnings of AU$1.74 billion for the first quarter of fiscal 2025, down from AU$1.8 billion for the year-earlier period. Cash earnings were down 2% compared with the quarterly average in the second half of fiscal 2024.

The bank's shares fell over 6% in recent trading.

data.title: BOJ's Next Rate Increase Now Likely to Occur in July — Market Talk
data.description: The Bank of Japan's next rate increase is likely to occur in July, two Mitsubishi UFJ Morgan Stanley Securities Debt Research members say in a report. The brokerage brings forward its forecasts, expecting a 25bp rate hike to 0.75% in July and another 25bp increase to 1.00% in January 2026. That compares with previous expectations for those moves to happen in October-December this year and October-December next year, respectively. It cites Japan's inflation having become stickier, a change in the BOJ's view of inflationary mechanisms, and the Ishiba cabinet wanting to keep the yen's depreciation and rising prices in check. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: United Overseas Bank Issues SG$3 Billion Package
data.description:
United Overseas Bank issued a SG$3 billion package as part of its capital distribution strategy for its shareholders, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

The package includes special dividends and share buybacks which will be delivered over the next three years.

A special dividend of SG$0.005 per share has been recommended, with payment to made in two tranches in 2025.

data.title: Australia shares tumble as NAB plummets; NZ gains after big rate cut
data.description:
Key points:
  • NAB falls as much as 8.6%, marks worst day since March 2020
  • CBA down 1.6%, set for fourth straight day of losses
  • Santos falls 2.8%, James Hardie rises up to 6%

Australian shares dropped on Wednesday, as National Australia Bank plunged the most in nearly five years on lower quarterly cash earnings, while New Zealand shares gained after its central bank cut interest rates as expected.

The S&P/ASX 200 index fell 0.6% to 8,434.5 points by 0158 GMT. The benchmark had slipped 0.7% on Tuesday.

In New Zealand, the benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index marginally extended gains, rising 0.2% to 13,071.99, after the Reserve Bank of New Zealand cut rates by 50 basis points, as was widely expected.

National Australia Bank , the country's second-biggest lender by market value, fell as much as 8.6% to A$36.130, its lowest since August 15, 2024 and the biggest intraday decline since March 23, 2020.

NAB's first-quarter cash earnings fell 3% to A$1.74 billion ($1.11 billion) as competition for customers ate into margins and more borrowers fell behind on repayments.

"Non-performing loan trends and mortgage arrears look slightly worse than peers," Citi analysts said.

NAB dragged financials down as much as 2.7%. Its bigger rival Commonwealth Bank of Australia also shed 1.6% in its fourth consecutive day of losses.

On Tuesday, the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) cut its cash rate for the first time in more than four years. The market is now pricing in a less than 20% chance of a rate cut in the RBA's April meeting, although brokerages are widely expecting another quarter-point cut in May.

Oil and gas explorer Santos slipped 2.8% after a 16% year-on-year drop in annual profit, while James Hardie jumped as much as 6% after the fibre cement maker's third-quarter profit beat expectations.

Overnight, the S&P 500 index squeaked past its previous record closing, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average and the Nasdaq Composite inched higher.

($1 = A$1.5743)

data.title: Rail Vikas Nigam JV Bags Project Worth INR5.5 Billion
data.description:
A joint venture of Rail Vikas Nigam has received a letter of acceptance from Rail Infrastructure Development Co. (Karnataka) for a project worth 5.54 billion Indian rupees, according to a Tuesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

RVNL owns 51% of the joint venture, while Rithwik Projects has the remaining 49% stake.

The project entails the construction of nine stations for the Bengaluru Suburban Rail Project in India, to be completed in 24 months.

data.title: Baidu's Hong Kong shares fall as quarterly revenue slips
data.description:
** Hong Kong shares of Chinese search engine group Baidu fall as much as 7.3% to HK$83.35, their lowest since February 3

** Stock last down 2.3%; fourth-biggest pct decliner in the Hang Seng Index , which is down 0.4%

** U.S.-listed stock fell 7.5% on Tuesday

** Baidu reported a 2% drop in quarterly revenue to 34.12 billion yuan ($4.7 billion) for three months through December, dragged down by a slowdown in the advertising business, though offset by gains from cloud AI

** Baidu said success of AI company DeepSeek had inspired it to embrace an open source model, under which the source code of its upcoming generative AI chatbot release will be publicly available

** Citi keeps "buy" rating, saying 4Q24 ads impact is seen bottoming out while cloud momentum is set to sustain into 2025

** Says it believes negative impact from genAI search transformation likely to be bottoming out in 4Q24 with sequential improvement of search ad revs starting from 2Q25 after seasonally soft 1Q25

** Jefferies maintains "buy", saying cloud growth faster than expected and it stands out with full stack of AI architecture

data.title: J.P. Morgan upgrades Singapore equities to overweight on strong outlook, target 6% gain
data.description:
J.P. Morgan has upgraded Singapore equities to overweight, citing attractive valuations, high dividend yields, and government measures aimed at revitalizing the domestic stock market.

The move follows Singapore’s latest budget announcement, which outlined initiatives to support economic growth and investment.

In a client note, J.P. Morgan analysts set a bullish target of 4,200 for the Straits Times Index (STI), representing a potential 6% gain from current levels. The benchmark index reached a record high of 3,949.65 on Wednesday and has risen 4% so far in 2025, following a 17% surge last year driven primarily by banking stocks and optimism around market-boosting policies.

Analysts expressed confidence in the government's strategy, stating that support for households and businesses would sustain economic activity, while investments in innovation and capital markets could unlock further growth opportunities. With improving market sentiment, Singapore’s equities may continue to benefit from both domestic and global investor interest.

data.title: Hartalega Holdings' Near-Term Outlook May Stay Weak — Market Talk
data.description: Hartalega Holdings' near-term outlook may remain weak, with fiscal 4Q sales volumes expected to decline sequentially as front-loading by U.S. buyers ahead of tariff hikes fades, CIMB Securities analyst Walter Aw Lik Hsin says in a note. Competitive pricing in non-U.S. markets and rising costs add further pressure to earnings, he reckons. The glove maker is prioritizing cost optimization, including workforce reductions and automation to mitigate challenges, he notes. Aw cuts Hartalega's FY 2025-FY 2027 EPS estimates by 37%-62% to factor in higher production costs and lower sales volume. CIMB downgrades Hartalega to hold from buy, cutting the target price to MYR2.70 from MYR4.20. Shares are 3.8% lower at MYR2.51.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Tesla steps up India hiring after Musk-Modi meet, fast-tracking market entry plan
data.description:
Elon Musk's Tesla is looking to hire senior staff in India, job advertisements posted this week showed, signalling progress in the electric vehicle maker's plans to enter the world's third-biggest auto market.

The postings come less than a week after Musk's meeting with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi in Washington.

Tesla did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.

Tesla has for years planned an India market entry and even setting up of local manufacturing. Some executives were hired around 2022, though some top-level people quit the company after Tesla put its India plans on hold back then.

With the automaker again scouting for showroom space in India since late last year, it has now listed 13 jobs on its website and social media platform LinkedIn for various customer-facing and back-end roles based in Mumbai, India's financial capital.

Among them is a "store manager" role responsible for overseeing and driving sales at the location, one job listing showed, a sign that Tesla is looking to open a showroom in the city. Delivery operations and customer support specialists are also being sought, the ads showed.

The listings come just after Modi met Musk in the United States last week, and discussed issues including space, mobility, technology, and innovation.

Musk has long criticised India for having high import tariffs of around 100% on EVs, and his company has repeatedly lobbied to relax them. The move, however, has faced opposition from local automakers who think Tesla's entry can hit their EV plans.

Last year, Musk was set to meet Modi during a trip to India where he was expected to announce a potential $2 billion-$3 billion investment, but the visit was called off after Tesla decided to lay off 10% of its global workforce amid declining sales.

In November, Reuters reported that Tesla was also scouting for showroom space in the country's capital city of New Delhi, and was in early stage talks with real estate developer DLF to help secure a location.

data.title: Market Chatter: South Korea's Naver, Kakao Intensify AI Battle Amid DeepSeek Disruption
data.description:
South Korea's two internet giants, Naver and Kakao , are ramping up AI competition as China's DeepSeek challenges market leaders, The Korea Herald reported.

Earlier this month, Kakao partnered with OpenAI to integrate ChatGPT technology into its services, including KakaoTalk and AI agent Kanana, according to a news release by Kakao.

Meanwhile, Naver is focusing on developing its own AI models for search, commerce, and content. Naver's AI strategy is expected to gain momentum with the return of founder Lee Hae-jin as a board member in March, according to The Korea Herald report.

Shares of Kakao rose 1% in recent trade on Wednesday.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Emmerson Resources Finds High-Grade Gold in Australian Project
data.description:
Emmerson Resources found high-grade gold mineralizations during extensional reverse circulation drilling at its White Devil gold project in Tennant Creek, Australia, according to a Wednesday Australian bourse filing.

The intersections yielded 33 meters at 5.1 grams per tonne of gold from 189 meters, including ten meters at 11.2 g/t gold.

The company has started development studies to determine if the project will be classified as a major mine deposit or a small mine deposit, the filing said.

data.title: Market Chatter: Chinese Animated Movie Ne Zha 2 Takes Top Animated Movie Spot
data.description:
Chinese animated movie Ne Zha 2 posted worldwide earnings exceeding 12.32 billion yuan ($1.72 billion) to become the world's highest-grossing animated movie, Xinhua News reported Thursday, citing data from ticketing platform Maoyan.

The previous record was held by Disney's 2024 movie Inside Out 2.

Maoyan and box office tracker Beacon estimated the film to exceed 15 billion yuan in domestic earnings, Xinhua News wrote.

data.title: China's Rail Passenger Trips Up 12% in January
data.description:
China's railways saw 369 million passenger trips in January, a 12% rise from a year earlier, with high-speed rail accounting for 74.6% of the total volume, according to data from the National Railway Administration.

High-speed rail alone handled 275 million trips, up 11% from last year.

Railways transported 423 million tonnes of cargo; freight turnover was 288.47 billion tonne-kilometers. Fixed-asset investment in railways grew 3.7% to 43.9 billion yuan.
